title: One thing I love about...HeroQuest!
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ofwkfFci1iI

Daniel (@dungeondive) distils his HeroQuest appreciation down to one thing: the board. That fixed dungeon layout with its corridors and rooms, the way furniture fills the spaces, the way players navigate a world that feels physical and permanent rather than randomly assembled from tiles. It's a short, focused love letter to a design choice most modern dungeon crawlers have abandoned.

Sometimes the simplest observation lands hardest. In a genre that's moved toward modular tiles and procedural generation, there's something to be said for a board that just sits there, waiting, every room already drawn.

----

What's the one design element in your favourite game that you think makes the whole thing work?
